skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction, polished implementation, and context-aware design choices.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents to build polished, memorable frontend interfaces.
skill.author: 1bertogit

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many weak-crypto matches, but review found only metadata, prose, and design guidance. The README command and network findings are documentation false positives. One low privacy concern remains because the sample plugin logs options and handler input.

low_findings.title: Unfiltered Input Logging
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The sample plugin logs initialization options and handler input. This is not malicious, but it could expose prompts or configuration data in logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The logging calls are direct and visible. Impact is limited because there is no network transmission or persistence shown.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis matched strings in filenames, metadata, descriptions, and design prose. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, cipher choice, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain only text, comments, identifiers, and examples. No crypto API or weak algorithm use appears in context.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fence Is Not Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: README lines 16 to 20 contain quoted natural-language prompt examples inside a Markdown code fence. They are not shell backticks and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The block contains example user prompts only. There is no Ruby code, shell command, or subprocess call.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link Is Not Runtime Network Access
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: README line 26 links to an external cookbook for learning material. It does not make a runtime request, send data, or load remote code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is a Markdown reference. No application code fetches it or transmits user data.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Design Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis matched ordinary descriptive text about frontend work and design systems. No host inspection, environment discovery, or operating system enumeration is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss interface quality, typography, and visual details. They do not call reconnaissance utilities or request system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unfiltered Input Logging
dangerous_patterns.description: The plugin sample logs raw options and input. Remove or redact these logs before using the plugin with sensitive prompts or configuration values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The console logging behavior is explicit. The risk depends on whether the sample plugin is used with sensitive data.

content.value_statement: Many AI-built interfaces look generic and forgettable. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward stronger visual direction and production-ready frontend output.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to choose a clear aesthetic direction before coding.
Supports frontend components, pages, applications, and interface requests.
Encourages polished HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, or similar implementation.
Provides direction for typography, color, motion, spacing, and visual atmosphere.
Helps avoid common AI design patterns and repeated default palettes.
limitations: It is guidance, not a full design system or component library.
It does not include bundled images, fonts, or ready-made UI assets.
Output quality depends on the agent, project stack, and user requirements.
Accessibility and browser testing still need separate verification.

use_cases.title: Design a Product Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create a focused dashboard with strong hierarchy, refined controls, and a clear visual direction.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Brand Landing Page
use_cases.description: Turn a product concept into a memorable landing page with tailored layout, typography, and motion.

use_cases.title: Refresh an Existing Interface
use_cases.description: Improve a dated screen with better spacing, stronger composition, and more intentional styling.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Simple Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished landing page for a personal finance app. Use a clear aesthetic direction and responsive HTML and CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for reusable UI pieces.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable settings panel with tabs, toggles, inputs, and empty states. Match a quiet professional SaaS style.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign an App Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for several connected views.
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign the onboarding flow for a collaborative notes app. Include states, transitions, mobile behavior, and implementation details.

prompt_templates.title: Build Within Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with stack and quality limits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React analytics dashboard using our existing component patterns. Avoid generic AI styling and include accessible interaction states.

output_examples.input: Create a dashboard for a music streaming app.
output_examples.output: A working dashboard concept with bold music-focused styling, listening metrics, playlist controls, responsive layout, and refined motion.

output_examples.input: Design a settings panel with dark mode.
output_examples.output: A compact settings interface with tabs, toggles, form states, dark theme choices, and clear spacing for repeated use.

output_examples.input: Build a landing page for an AI security startup.
output_examples.output: A distinctive product page with strong visual hierarchy, security-focused tone, responsive sections, and production-ready styling.

best_practices: Give the agent the audience, product purpose, and technical stack.
Ask for working code plus responsive and interactive states.
Review the result for accessibility, performance, and brand fit.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for only a beautiful design without functional requirements.
Do not accept repeated generic palettes, layouts, or font choices.
Do not ship generated interfaces without browser and accessibility checks.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components, pages, and applications with stronger visual direction and polished implementation.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It guides the agent toward production-grade code, but teams should still review, test, and integrate the result.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include a component library?
faq.answer: No. It is a design and implementation guidance skill, not a packaged UI library.

faq.question: Can it follow an existing brand system?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide brand rules, components, colors, and constraints in the prompt.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only low risk, mainly sample plugin logging and a documentation link.
